name: Saltimbocca Alla Genovese (from Liguria Area)
recipeIngredient: Fresh sage or dried sage or Ground sage
1 cup Marsala or dry white wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the veal scallops so that they are approximately all the same size. Save trimmings for sauces.
2. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper on each slice of meat.
3. Place 2 or more leaves of fresh or dried sage on each veal slice, or sprinkle about a scant 1/4 teaspoon ground sage on each.
4. Cut prosciutto to the same size as veal slices.
5. Top each veal slice with a prosciutto slice.
6. Roll up and secure with toothpicks or tie with kitchen thread.
7. Coat each veal roll lightly with flour, shaking off excess flour.
8. Divide butter between 2 large frying pans; heat it without browning.
9. Over medium heat, cook veal rolls about 5 minutes, or until golden brown on all sides.
10. Reduce heat and add 1/2 cup Marsala and 1/4 cup beef bouillon to each frying pan.
11. Cover frying pans and cook for 5 to 10 minutes longer; cooking time depends on the size and thickness of the rolls.
12. Shake the pans frequently to prevent sticking.
13. If necessary, add a little more beef bouillon; there should be 1 to 2 tablespoons sauce for each veal bird.
14. Transfer the cooked veal birds to a heated serving dish and spoon the sauce over them.
15. Serve immediately and very hot with a tossed green salad.
